steps.title: Trace the frying mechanism
steps.content: The passage gives a cause-and-effect chain: water evaporates from the crust, voids form, oil fills those voids, and fat content increases. If less moisture leaves the food, fewer voids are available for oil.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported effects
steps.content: Choice B introduces frying temperature, which the passage does not connect to moisture loss. Choice C concerns how fast the crust loses permeability, which is not stated. Choice D introduces crispiness, another unsupported outcome. The answer is A.
